articles.context: The United Nations Economic Commission for Africa (UNECA) plays a crucial role in promoting economic development and cooperation in the Horn of Africa, particularly amid Ethiopia's ongoing political tensions following the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020. As the region seeks stability and recovery, the EOI reflects UNECA's efforts to engage stakeholders in rebuilding economic frameworks and fostering regional partnerships, particularly in light of the African Continental Free Trade Area agreement established in January 2021.

As the country grapples with economic instability and displacement, organizations like Save the Children are crucial in providing essential services. The tender for statutory audit services is significant as it ensures accountability and transparency in the management of funds aimed at addressing these urgent needs.

As the Ethiopian government and Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) reached a peace agreement in November 2022, the IRC's ongoing efforts are crucial for recovery and rebuilding in the affected regions, especially in light of the severe drought conditions impacting the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: Constitution review body launches civic education for national institutions
articles.summary: South Sudan’s constitution-making process entered a new phase on Monday after the National Constitution Review The post Constitution review body launches civic education for national institutions appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: South Sudan has been grappling with political instability since its independence in 2011, exacerbated by a civil war that erupted in 2013. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to establish a transitional government and promote peace. The launch of civic education by the National Constitution Review Commission is a crucial step in fostering public engagement and ensuring that the new constitution reflects the diverse interests of the nation amidst ongoing challenges.

articles.context: As Egypt prepares to face Belgium in a friendly match, the celebration of Mohamed Salah's birthday highlights the national pride surrounding the star player amidst ongoing challenges in the region. The match, scheduled for October 16, 2023, serves as a morale booster for the Egyptian team and its fans, especially in light of recent political tensions and economic difficulties in Sudan and neighboring countries, which have affected regional stability and sports diplomacy.

articles.context: تأتي مباراة مصر وبلجيكا في كأس العالم 2026 في ظل توترات سياسية واقتصادية في المنطقة، حيث يسعى المنتخب المصري لتعزيز صورته بعد الأزمات الداخلية. كما أن الأداء الجيد في المونديال قد يساهم في توحيد الشعب المصري في وقت تتزايد فيه التحديات، بما في ذلك الأزمات الاقتصادية منذ عام 2011. تعتبر هذه المباراة فرصة لتعزيز الروح الوطنية في ظل الظروف الراهنة.

articles.title: أزمة تمويل حادة تواجه “العائدين” من السودان إلى تشاد
articles.summary: يتواصل تدفّق التشاديين “العائدين” الفارّين من النزاع في السودان إلى معبر أدري الحدودي في تشاد، لكن الدعم الحيوي الذي تقدّمه وكالات تابعة للأمم المتحدة كالمنظمة الدولية للهجرة قد يتوقف في أي لحظة بسبب نقص التمويل. في السودان المجاور، أدّت الحرب الأهلية الدائرة منذ أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، إلى سقوط عشرات آلاف … The post أزمة تمويل حادة تواجه “العائدين” من السودان إلى تشاد appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تتفاقم أزمة النزوح من السودان منذ اندلاع الحرب الأهلية في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى مقتل عشرات الآلاف وتهجير الملايين. تشهد تشاد، التي تستضيف العديد من اللاجئين، نقصًا حادًا في التمويل لوكالات الإغاثة، مما يهدد قدرة هذه الوكالات على تقديم الدعم الحيوي للعائدين. هذه الديناميكية تعكس التوترات الإقليمية وتأثير النزاع السوداني على الاستقرار في دول الجوار.

articles.title: الإعلانات الحكومية في السودان.. مكافأة للموالين وسلاح صامت لتصفية المنابر الحرة
articles.summary: يواجه المشهد الإعلامي السوداني المنقسم بفعل الحرب منعطفاً جديداً في يونيو 2026 ، بعد صدور قرار وزارة الثقافة والإعلام الاتحادية القاضي بإلزام كافة المواقع والمنصات والصحف الإلكترونية بالتسجيل وتوفيق أوضاعها الرسمي. هذا الإجراء، الذي بررته السلطات بـ “التنظيم والضبط”، واجه جبهة رفض قوية وموحدة من نقابة الصحفيين السودانيين والأجسام المهنية. واعتبرت هذه الجهات الخطوة محاولة … The post الإعلانات الحكومية في السودان.. مكافأة للموالين وسلاح صامت لتصفية المنابر الحرة appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تأتي هذه الخطوة في سياق تصاعد التوترات السياسية في السودان بعد اندلاع الحرب الأهلية في عام 2023، والتي أدت إلى تفكك الحكومة المركزية وزيادة الانقسامات بين الفصائل المسلحة. كما أن قرار وزارة الثقافة والإعلام يعكس جهود النظام للسيطرة على المعلومات وتوجيهها، في ظل رفض نقابة الصحفيين السودانيين، التي تسعى لحماية حرية التعبير في مواجهة القمع المتزايد.

articles.title: ياسمين عبد العزيز والسقا يعودان من قبرص بعد تصوير عدة مشاهد أكشن ومطاردات
articles.summary: عاد النجمان ياسمين عبد العزيز وأحمد السقا إلى القاهرة بعد الانتهاء من تصوير عدد من المشاهد الخارجية لفيلمهما الجديد «خلي بالك من نفسك» في قبرص، والتي استغرقت نحو 5 أيام متواصلة. وشهدت رحلة التصوير تنفيذ عدد من مشاهد المطاردات والأكشن التي تُعد من أبرز محطات الفيلم، حيث حرص صناع العمل على تصويرها في مواقع متنوعة …
articles.context: تأتي عودة ياسمين عبد العزيز وأحمد السقا إلى القاهرة في وقت يشهد فيه قطاع السينما في مصر انتعاشًا بعد تأثيرات جائحة كورونا، حيث تسعى الصناعة لاستعادة نشاطها. كما أن الفيلم "خلي بالك من نفسك" يُعتبر جزءًا من جهود تعزيز التعاون الثقافي بين مصر وقبرص، مما يعكس العلاقات المتنامية بين البلدين في مجالات الفن والترفيه.

articles.title: متحدث «قمم»: انتهاكات الجيش بتحريض إخواني لإذلال السودانيين
articles.summary: أعرب الناطق الرسمي باسم القوى المدنية المتحدة بالسودان “قمم” عثمان عبد الرحمن سليمان عن قلقه من تصاعد الانتهاكات الخطيرة بحق السودانيين في مناطق سيطرة الجيش. وقال سليمان إن القوى المدنية المتحدة تتابع بقلق بالغ ما تشهده تلك المناطق من “تصاعد خطير في الجرائم والانتهاكات”، مشيرًا إلى تداول مقاطع فيديو موثقة تُظهر مواطنين وهم يتعرضون للضرب … The post متحدث «قمم»: انتهاكات الجيش بتحريض إخواني لإذلال السودانيين appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تأتي تصريحات عثمان عبد الرحمن سليمان في وقت يشهد فيه السودان تصاعدًا في التوترات بين الجيش وقوى الثورة، خاصة بعد اندلاع النزاع في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع. هذه الانتهاكات تُعتبر جزءًا من الصراع الأوسع الذي يواجهه السودان منذ الإطاحة بنظام عمر البشير في 2019، حيث تتزايد المخاوف من تأثير الجماعات الإسلامية المتطرفة على الوضع الأمني والسياسي في البلاد.

articles.title: الإخوان والبرهان..الكتلة الإسلامية بوابة العودة لحكم السودان
articles.summary: يعكف تنظيم الإخوان المسلمين في السودان على إطلاق كيان جديد، في محاولة تُعد الثامنة من نوعها لإعادة التموضع السياسي منذ تأسيسه قبل نحو 8 عقود، وذلك في ظل ضغوط متصاعدة يواجهها التنظيم بعد تصنيفه جماعة إرهابية في مارس 2026. وتأتي هذه الخطوة ضمن مساعٍ مستمرة لإعادة إنتاج حضوره في المشهد السياسي عبر واجهات وتحالفات جديدة، … The post الإخوان والبرهان..الكتلة الإسلامية بوابة العودة لحكم السودان appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تأسس تنظيم الإخوان المسلمين في السودان عام 1949، وقد واجه تحديات كبيرة بعد الإطاحة بنظام عمر البشير في 2019. تصنيف التنظيم كجماعة إرهابية في مارس 2026 زاد من ضغوطه السياسية، مما دفعه لمحاولة إعادة تشكيل نفسه عبر كيان جديد. تأتي هذه الخطوة في وقت تشهد فيه البلاد صراعات سياسية مستمرة، خاصة مع تصاعد التوترات بين الجيش بقيادة عبد الفتاح البرهان والقوى المدنية.

articles.title: نزوح جديد في شمال دارفور بعد هجمات على كرنوي وأمبرو
articles.summary: كشفت أربعة مصادر متطابقة من ولاية شمال دارفور، الأحد، عن هجوم مسلح نفذته قوات الدعم السريع على قرى منطقة خزان أورشي بمحلية أمبرو، شمال غرب الفاشر، ما أدى إلى مقتل وجرح العشرات من السكان ونزوح آخرين نحو بلدة الطينة. وقال هارون خاطر، أحد القيادات الأهلية بشمال دارفور، لـ”دارفور24″، إن قوات الدعم السريع شنت، الأحد، هجمات … The post نزوح جديد في شمال دارفور بعد هجمات على كرنوي وأمبرو appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تأتي هذه الهجمات في سياق تصاعد التوترات في شمال دارفور، حيث تتنافس قوات الدعم السريع، التي تشكلت في عام 2013، مع القوات الحكومية والمجموعات المسلحة الأخرى. في عام 2020، تم توقيع اتفاق سلام بين الحكومة السودانية وبعض الحركات المسلحة، لكن الوضع الأمني لا يزال هشًا، مما أدى إلى تجدد النزوح والاعتداءات على المدنيين. الهجمات الأخيرة على كرنوي وأمبرو تعكس استمرار عدم الاستقرار في المنطقة.

articles.title: حزب الأمة القومي ينعى ضحايا فاجعة غرق قارب مهاجرين من أبناء السديرة الشرقية
articles.summary: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم حزب الأمة القومي الأمانة العامة قال تعالى: {وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُم بِشَيْءٍ مِّنَ الْخَوْفِ وَالْجُوعِ وَنَقْصٍ مِّنَ الْأَمْوَالِ وَالْأَنفُسِ وَالثَّمَرَاتِ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ الصَّابِرِينَ ۝ الَّذِينَ إِذَا أَصَابَتْهُم مُّصِيبَةٌ قَالُوا إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ} صدق الله العظيم. ينعى حزب الأمة القومي، ببالغ الحزن والأسى، مجموعة من أبناء الوطن من منطقة السديرة الشرقية بمحلية الكاملين، … The post حزب الأمة القومي ينعى ضحايا فاجعة غرق قارب مهاجرين من أبناء السديرة الشرقية appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تأتي هذه الفاجعة في سياق الأزمة الإنسانية المتزايدة في السودان، حيث يعاني العديد من المواطنين من النزوح والفقر نتيجة النزاعات المستمرة منذ عام 2019. كما أن غرق القارب يعكس المخاطر التي يواجهها المهاجرون في البحث عن حياة أفضل، وسط غياب الاستقرار السياسي والاقتصادي. يُعتبر حزب الأمة القومي أحد الأحزاب الرئيسية في السودان، ويعبر عن قلقه إزاء هذه المآسي التي تصيب أبناء الوطن.

articles.title: South Sudanese women struggle for justice in male-dominated system
articles.summary: As she prepared to give birth to her first child at Melikia Hospital in Juba The post South Sudanese women struggle for justice in male-dominated system appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: South Sudan has been grappling with a protracted civil war since its independence in 2011, exacerbating gender-based violence and limiting women's access to justice. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to address these issues, yet implementation has been slow, leaving women vulnerable in a patriarchal legal system. Recent reports indicate that the ongoing instability continues to hinder efforts to empower women and protect their rights.

articles.title: روسيا تخسر حرب أوكرانيا… ويأس بوتين ينذر بمخاطر جسيمة

articles.context: تأتي هذه المقالة في وقت حرج حيث تواصل الحرب في أوكرانيا التأثير على العلاقات الدولية، بما في ذلك في منطقة القرن الأفريقي. منذ الغزو الروسي في فبراير 2022، زادت المخاوف من تصاعد التوترات، خاصة مع دعم الدول الغربية لأوكرانيا. في السودان، حيث يواجه النظام تحديات داخلية، قد تؤدي هزيمة بوتين إلى تغييرات في الديناميات الإقليمية، مما يؤثر على التحالفات السياسية والاقتصادية في المنطقة.

articles.title: Somalia receives modern equipment from Türkiye to boost farming
articles.summary: Turkish support to help Somalia achieve food self-sufficiency, says Somali minister
articles.context: Somalia has faced decades of civil war and political instability, which have severely impacted its agricultural sector. The recent partnership with Türkiye, formalized during President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan's visit in 2021, aims to enhance Somalia's food security amid ongoing droughts and humanitarian crises. This support aligns with Somalia's National Development Plan, which emphasizes self-sufficiency and agricultural development as critical to stabilizing the nation and reducing dependency on foreign aid.

articles.title: IFRC condemns killing of Sudanese Red Crescent volunteer in North Kordofan
articles.summary: The International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies (IFRC) on Saturday condemned the The post IFRC condemns killing of Sudanese Red Crescent volunteer in North Kordofan appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The killing of the Sudanese Red Crescent volunteer occurs amid ongoing violence in Sudan, particularly following the outbreak of conflict in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces. This conflict has exacerbated humanitarian crises, with aid workers increasingly targeted. The IFRC's condemnation highlights the dangers faced by humanitarian personnel in a country already struggling with political instability and a fractured peace process since the ousting of former President Omar al-Bashir in 2019.

articles.title: من إنجاز مونديال قطر إلى حلم 2026: كيف تحول المغرب إلى قوة كروية عالمية؟
articles.summary: لم يكن أكثر المتفائلين من المغاربة، سواء من الجيل الذي عاش إنجاز مونديال المكسيك عام 1986 حين بلغ المنتخب المغربي الدور ثمن النهائي، أو من جيلي الذي عاش نهائي كأس أفريقيا عام 2004، يتوقع أن يصبح المغرب رقماً صعباً في عالم كرة القدم بعد بلوغه نصف نهائي كأس العالم 2022 في قطر. الاستثمار في البنيات …
articles.context: تأتي إنجازات المغرب في كرة القدم في سياق تاريخي معقد، حيث شهدت المنطقة توترات سياسية وصراعات مثل النزاع حول الصحراء الغربية. بعد تأهل المغرب لنصف نهائي كأس العالم 2022، أصبح الحديث عن تعزيز الهوية الوطنية من خلال الرياضة أكثر أهمية، خاصة في ظل الأزمات الاقتصادية والاجتماعية التي تعاني منها دول شمال إفريقيا. كما أن نجاح المنتخب المغربي يعكس تطور العلاقات الرياضية بين الدول العربية والإفريقية.

articles.context: تأتي هذه الأحداث في نيالا في ظل تصاعد التوترات القبلية في دارفور، حيث تتصارع عدة فصائل مسلحة منذ اندلاع النزاع في عام 2003. يُعتبر الدعم السريع، الذي تأسس في عام 2013، أحد الفاعلين الرئيسيين في الصراع، ويواجه اتهامات بانتهاكات حقوق الإنسان. كما أن الاحتجاجات الحالية تعكس استياء قبيلة بني هلبة من النظام السابق، الذي قاد البلاد حتى الإطاحة به في عام 2019، مما يزيد من تعقيد الوضع الأمني والسياسي في المنطقة.

articles.context: تأتي عودة الحكم الصومالي عمر أرتان إلى بلاده في وقت حساس حيث تسعى الصومال لتعزيز مكانتها في المجتمع الدولي، خاصة بعد استعادة الحكومة الفيدرالية السيطرة على مناطق كانت تحت سيطرة حركة الشباب. كما أن منع أرتان من دخول الولايات المتحدة يثير تساؤلات حول سياسة الهجرة الأمريكية وتأثيرها على الرياضيين من الدول ذات الخلفيات المتوترة، مما يعكس التوترات السياسية القائمة في المنطقة.

articles.title: $80M IFC Investment to Fund Sheraton Addis Revamp and Launch Ethiopia’s First Green-Certified Hotels
articles.summary: The International Finance Corporation (IFC) has announced a landmark partnership with MIDROC Ethiopia PLC (MEP) to invest $80 million in the country’s premium hospitality (guest reception) sector. It was indicated that the project, backed by a long-term loan from the IFC, aims to expand upscale hotel capacity, bolster the national tourism ecosystem, and drive high, […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's tourism sector has faced significant challenges due to the ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which escalated in November 2020, leading to a decline in international visitors. The government's efforts to stabilize the situation and promote economic recovery have intensified, making this $80 million investment by the IFC crucial for revitalizing the hospitality industry and enhancing Ethiopia's appeal as a tourist destination amidst ongoing political reforms and recovery efforts.

articles.title: Rest In Peace!! 8 Suspects In The Murder Of Sydney Gongodyo Identified & Arrested
articles.summary: Authorities have confirmed the arrest of eight suspects in connection with the killing of rugby player Sydney Gongodyo, The post Rest In Peace!! 8 Suspects In The Murder Of Sydney Gongodyo Identified & Arrested appeared first on The Nile Post .
articles.context: The murder of Sydney Gongodyo, a prominent rugby player in South Sudan, highlights the ongoing violence and instability in the country, exacerbated by the civil war that began in 2013. The recent peace agreement signed in 2018 aimed to end years of conflict, yet incidents like Gongodyo's killing reveal the challenges in achieving lasting security. His death has sparked outrage among sports communities and advocates for justice, emphasizing the urgent need for effective law enforcement in South Sudan.

articles.title: IFC Extends USD 80mln Loan to MIDROC for Sheraton Addis Expansion, Renovation
articles.summary: The International Finance Corporation (IFC), the private-sector lending arm of the World Bank Group, has agreed to provide an USD 80 million long-term loan to MIDROC Investment Group to finance the renovation of the Sheraton Addis hotel and the construction of a new Sheraton-branded property in Ethiopia. The financing agreement was signed in Nairobi by […]
articles.context: The loan comes at a critical time as Ethiopia seeks to revitalize its tourism sector following the impacts of the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020. The Ethiopian government has been working to attract foreign investment to stimulate economic recovery, and the Sheraton Addis, a symbol of luxury and stability, plays a key role in this strategy. MIDROC, owned by Ethiopian-Saudi businessman Mohammed Hussein Al Amoudi, has been pivotal in the country's economic landscape.

articles.title: Hero’s welcome for Somali referee denied US entry for World Cup
articles.summary: 'Despite the circumstances, I am in a positive mood and I am focused on the next challenges,' says Omar Artan
articles.context: Omar Artan, a Somali referee, was denied entry to the United States for the 2026 World Cup qualifiers due to visa issues, highlighting ongoing challenges faced by Somali nationals amid complex immigration policies. This incident occurs against the backdrop of Somalia's efforts to rebuild its international reputation following decades of civil conflict and instability, particularly since the 2012 formation of the Federal Government, which has been striving to enhance the country’s global engagement through sports.

articles.title: Somali referee returns home via Istanbul after US entry denial
articles.summary: 'I wanted to thank FIFA for supporting me all the way and the Somali people also,' Omar Abdulkadir Artan says at Istanbul Airport
articles.context: Omar Abdulkadir Artan, a Somali referee, faced entry denial to the U.S. amid heightened scrutiny of immigration policies affecting individuals from conflict-affected regions. His return via Istanbul underscores the ongoing challenges faced by Somali nationals, particularly in the context of the 2020 U.S. travel ban affecting several Muslim-majority countries. Artan's situation highlights FIFA's commitment to supporting sports figures from Somalia, a nation still grappling with instability and the aftermath of civil war since the early 1990s.

articles.title: NEBE Releases Preliminary Results From 24 Constituencies as Prosperity Party Dominates
articles.summary: The National Election Board of Ethiopia (NEBE) has released preliminary results from 24 constituencies, showing the ruling Prosperity Party winning all but one of the seats announced so far in federal parliamentary and regional council races. The sole opposition victory was recorded in Gondar Zuria Constituency No. 4 in the Amhara region, where Tahir Mohammed […]
articles.context: The preliminary results come amidst heightened political tensions in Ethiopia following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The Prosperity Party, led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, has faced criticism for its handling of ethnic tensions and governance. The elections are seen as a critical test of the party's legitimacy and stability in a country still grappling with the aftermath of civil unrest and regional power struggles.

articles.title: Somali Football Federation voices deep regret after referee denied entry to US
articles.summary: Omar Artan is source of pride for Somalia, African football, says Federation
articles.context: The Somali Football Federation's disappointment stems from the recent denial of entry to referee Omar Artan by U.S. authorities, highlighting ongoing challenges faced by Somali athletes in international contexts. This incident occurs against the backdrop of Somalia's efforts to rebuild its sports infrastructure following decades of civil conflict, particularly since the establishment of the Federal Government in 2012, which has sought to enhance national representation in global sports events.

articles.title: Somali referee 'disappointed' after being denied US entry ahead of FIFA World Cup
articles.summary: ‘I had the right papers and everything. I had the right visa,’ says Omar Abdulkadir Artan
articles.context: Omar Abdulkadir Artan's denial of entry to the U.S. comes amid heightened scrutiny of visa policies affecting individuals from Somalia, a country still grappling with the aftermath of decades of civil war and instability. The ongoing political challenges, including the recent 2022 elections and the struggle against Al-Shabaab insurgents, have complicated international perceptions of Somali citizens. Artan's situation highlights the broader issues of representation and opportunity for Somali athletes on the global stage.

articles.title: Who is the Somali referee barred from entering the US for the World Cup?
articles.summary: Omar Artan was set to become the first Somali to referee a game at the World Cup finals, but his place in history has been denied by US immigration authorities.
articles.context: Omar Artan's barring from the US comes amid heightened scrutiny of immigration policies following the Trump administration's travel bans, which have disproportionately affected individuals from predominantly Muslim countries, including Somalia. This incident highlights ongoing challenges faced by Somali nationals, particularly in the context of the country's fragile political situation, where instability and conflict have persisted since the early 1990s, impacting opportunities for representation in global events like the World Cup.

articles.title: Man reportedly shot at Kenya protest against US Ebola quarantine centre
articles.summary: Protesters are concerned about cross-border infection risks and the lack of transparency from the government about the treatment centre.
articles.context: The protest in Kenya comes amid heightened fears of Ebola outbreaks in the region, particularly following the 2014 West African epidemic that claimed thousands of lives. The Kenyan government's decision to establish a US-funded Ebola quarantine center has sparked controversy, as critics argue it lacks transparency and could exacerbate local tensions. This situation is further complicated by Kenya's ongoing struggles with public trust in government health initiatives, particularly after the COVID-19 pandemic response.

articles.title: I have right papers and visa - barred referee Artan
articles.summary: Somali referee Omar Artan says he was subjected to an 11-hour immigration interview before being denied entry to the United States for the World Cup despite holding the "right papers" and "right visa".
articles.context: Omar Artan's denial of entry to the U.S. comes amid heightened scrutiny of immigration policies following the Trump administration's travel bans, which disproportionately affected individuals from Muslim-majority countries, including Somalia. The ongoing instability in Somalia, exacerbated by the rise of militant groups like Al-Shabaab, has led to increased diaspora engagement in international events, making Artan's situation emblematic of broader challenges faced by Somali athletes seeking global recognition.

articles.title: ECMA Licenses First Foreign Investment Bank in Ethiopia
articles.summary: Subsidiary of Nigeria’s United Capital Group Enters Market Ethiopia’s capital markets regulator has granted an investment banking license to a Nigerian financial services firm, marking the first time a foreign investment bank has established a subsidiary in the country and signaling growing foreign interest in Ethiopia’s nascent capital market. The Ethiopian Capital Market Authority (ECMA) […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's capital market has been largely undeveloped due to years of political instability and conflict, particularly following the Tigray War that began in November 2020. The establishment of the Ethiopian Capital Market Authority in 2021 aimed to attract foreign investment and stimulate economic growth. The licensing of Nigeria's United Capital Group as the first foreign investment bank in Ethiopia represents a significant step towards integrating Ethiopia into the global financial system amidst ongoing economic reforms.

articles.title: Central Bank Postpones FX Auction Amid Technical Issues
articles.summary: The National Bank of Ethiopia (NBE) has postponed its latest foreign-exchange auction, citing “unforeseen technical issues.” The central bank had announced on May 27 that it would conduct two foreign-exchange auctions of USD 100 million each during the fourth quarter of the fiscal year. The amount marked a sharp reduction from the previous auction, in […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's foreign-exchange market has faced significant pressure due to ongoing economic challenges, including inflation and currency devaluation. The NBE's decision to reduce the auction amount follows the government's efforts to stabilize the economy amid the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which has strained resources and disrupted trade. This auction postponement comes just weeks after the government announced a new economic reform plan aimed at attracting foreign investment and boosting foreign reserves.

articles.title: Council of Ministers approves over 2.3 trillion birr draft budget for 2026/27
articles.summary: In its 56th regular session held today, the Council of Ministers unanimously approved a historic 2.34 trillion Birr draft federal government budget for the upcoming 2026/2027 FY. The Council has referred this unanimously approved draft budget proclamation to the House of Peoples’ Representatives for a final decision. The newly proposed budget significantly increases the government’s […]
articles.context: The approval of the 2.34 trillion Birr budget comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing recovery from the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The budget aims to address pressing economic challenges, including inflation and infrastructure deficits, while also reflecting the government's commitment to national reconciliation and development. This financial plan is critical as Ethiopia navigates its post-conflict landscape and seeks to stabilize its economy.

articles.title: Arrests, tear gas as fresh protests erupt outside US Ebola facility in Kenya
articles.summary: Businesses shut in central Kenyan town as residents oppose quarantine center at Laikipia Air Base
articles.context: The protests in Laikipia come amid heightened tensions following the Kenyan government's decision to establish a quarantine center for Ebola at the Laikipia Air Base, announced in early October 2023. This move has sparked fears among residents about potential health risks and economic impacts, particularly as the region has faced previous conflicts over land and resource allocation. The ongoing struggle between local communities and government authorities adds to the significance of these demonstrations.

articles.title: What happened and why?
articles.summary: The first Somali set to referee at a World Cup finals has been dropped from the list of officials after he was denied entry to the United States.
articles.context: The decision to deny entry to the Somali referee comes amid ongoing tensions between the U.S. and Somalia, particularly following the Biden administration's renewed focus on counterterrorism efforts in the region. This situation is exacerbated by the Somali government's struggle to maintain stability amidst the resurgence of Al-Shabaab, which has led to increased scrutiny of Somali nationals traveling abroad. The referee's exclusion highlights the broader challenges faced by Somali representatives on the international stage.

articles.title: Somali referee Artan barred from entering US
articles.summary: Omar Artan, who was set to be the first Somali to referee at the World Cup finals, is dropped from the list of officials after he was denied entry to the United States.
articles.context: Omar Artan's exclusion from the World Cup comes amid heightened scrutiny of immigration policies in the U.S., particularly affecting individuals from countries with ongoing conflicts like Somalia. The Somali government has been working to improve its international image and promote sports as a unifying force, especially after the 2017 agreement with FIFA to enhance football development in the country. Artan's situation underscores the challenges faced by Somali athletes on the global stage.

articles.title: Cabinet Forwards Record 2.34tln Birr Federal Budget Proposal to Parliament
articles.summary: The Council of Ministers has approved a proposal for a 2.34 trillion Birr budget for the 2026/27 fiscal year (EFY 2019) and forwarded it to Parliament for review, according to a statement issued by the Office of the Prime Minister today. Despite it being nearly half a trillion Birr more than the 1.9 trillion Birr […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's proposed 2.34 trillion Birr budget comes amid ongoing political tensions following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. As the country seeks to stabilize and rebuild, the budget reflects the government's priorities in addressing economic recovery and social services. Additionally, the proposal's significant increase over the previous budget highlights the urgent need for funding in a context of rising inflation and humanitarian challenges.

articles.title: Central Bank Nods to Fuel Imports by Foreign Investors, Embassies, International Orgs
articles.summary: Regulators at the National Bank of Ethiopia (NBE) are allowing diplomatic missions, foreign investors, and international organizations to bypass the Ethiopian Petroleum Supply Enterprise (EPSE) and import fuel for their own consumption. The change was introduced in the newly amended Franco Valuta Directive, which frees these organizations from reliance on state-controlled fuel supply through EPSE […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant fuel shortages exacerbated by the ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which began in November 2020, disrupting supply chains and economic stability. The government’s tight control over fuel distribution through the Ethiopian Petroleum Supply Enterprise has led to widespread frustration among foreign entities operating in the country. The recent amendment to the Franco Valuta Directive, effective as of October 2023, reflects a shift towards easing these restrictions amid growing economic pressures.

articles.title: Blow for Kenya's ex-deputy president as court upholds his impeachment
articles.summary: The decision weakens Rigathi Gachagua's bid for the presidency, although his lawyers have vowed to challenge the ruling.
articles.context: Rigathi Gachagua, who served as Deputy President under William Ruto since September 2022, faced impeachment due to allegations of corruption and abuse of office. This ruling comes amid heightened political tensions following the contentious 2022 elections, where Ruto's administration has struggled with economic challenges and public discontent. Gachagua's legal battle reflects broader issues of accountability and governance in Kenya, particularly in the wake of the 2010 Constitution that aimed to enhance checks and balances.

articles.title: Foreign Ministry Concludes Diplomatic Training Program for South Sudanese Officials
articles.summary: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs has completed a five-day diplomatic training program for South Sudanese diplomats, an initiative officials said was aimed at strengthening professional capacity and deepening ties between the neighboring countries. The program, organized by the Ministry and the Institute of Foreign Affairs was held in Addis Ababa from June 5 to 9 […]
articles.context: The diplomatic training program comes amid ongoing efforts to stabilize relations between Ethiopia and South Sudan following years of conflict and political instability in South Sudan, exacerbated by the civil war that began in 2013. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan has prompted regional actors, including Ethiopia, to support South Sudan's governance capacity, making this training initiative a crucial step in fostering diplomatic ties and regional cooperation.

articles.title: Ethiopian Airlines Braces for Revenue Dip in Face of Rising Fuel Costs
articles.summary: Ethiopian Airlines expects its profit margins to shrink this year as soaring jet fuel prices linked to conflict in the Middle East drive up operating costs and weaken demand on some routes. Speaking on the sidelines of the annual meeting of the International Air Transport Association (IATA) in Rio de Janeiro this week, CEO Mesfin […]
articles.context: Ethiopian Airlines, Africa's largest airline, is facing challenges amid escalating jet fuel prices due to the ongoing Israel-Palestine conflict, which has disrupted oil supply chains. This situation is exacerbated by Ethiopia's internal political tensions, particularly the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which has strained the economy and reduced travel demand. The airline's financial health is crucial as it plays a vital role in connecting the Horn of Africa to global markets.

articles.title: Kenya's ex-chief justice arrested at protest against building on national park
articles.summary: Activists allege there is a plan to build a car park on protected land belonging to a wildlife sanctuary.
articles.context: The arrest of former Chief Justice David Maraga at the protest highlights ongoing tensions between conservation efforts and urban development in Kenya. In 2020, the Nairobi National Park faced threats from proposed infrastructure projects, including a controversial road expansion plan. The Kenya Wildlife Service has been under pressure to protect the park, which is home to diverse wildlife, amid rising demands for land due to rapid urbanization and population growth in Nairobi.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s First State-Owned Enterprise Listing on the ESX Puts National Wealth in the Hands of Everyday Citizens
articles.summary: #Advertorial Ethiopia has marked a significant transition in its financial landscape. With the official listing of Ethio telecom on the Main Market of the Ethiopian Securities Exchange (ESX) under the ticker symbol TELE, ordinary citizens now have the opportunity to directly own shares in one of the nation’s most valuable enterprises. With 45,366 retail shareholders […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's move to list Ethio telecom on the Ethiopian Securities Exchange comes amid ongoing economic reforms initiated by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed since 2018, aimed at liberalizing the state-dominated economy. This listing is particularly significant as it follows the 2020 signing of the Abiy-led peace agreement with the Tigray People's Liberation Front, which has since allowed for greater financial stability and investor confidence in a country recovering from civil conflict.

articles.title: SADC launches landmark initiative to build sustainable energy transition mineral value chains
articles.summary: The Southern African Development Community (SADC) region has many of the sought after critical energy transition minerals. Now a five-year regional project is working to retain the mineral wealth locally while promoting livelihoods and driving industrialization. The SADC region holds vast reserves of cobalt, lithium, manganese, nickel, and platinum group metals which have for many […]
articles.context: The SADC initiative comes at a crucial time as global demand for energy transition minerals surges, driven by the 2021 COP26 climate commitments. Ethiopia, a member of SADC, is navigating internal conflicts and political instability, which complicate its participation in regional economic initiatives. Moreover, the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA), launched in 2021, aims to enhance intra-African trade, making this initiative pivotal for Ethiopia's economic recovery and regional cooperation.

articles.title: Africa Financial Summit 2026 chooses Luanda to continue its mission of integrating African finance
articles.summary: The Africa Financial Summit – AFIS, the leading platform for African finance, announces that its annual summit will be held on November 3 and 4, 2026, in Luanda, Angola. For the first time, the summit will take place in Southern Africa, confirming its pan-African reach and its ambition to connect all of the continent’s financial […]
articles.context: The choice of Luanda for the Africa Financial Summit 2026 is significant amid ongoing efforts to bolster economic cooperation in the region, particularly following the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) agreement in 2021. This summit comes at a time when Ethiopia is navigating internal conflicts and seeking to strengthen its economic ties with neighboring countries, highlighting the importance of regional financial integration for stability and growth across the Horn of Africa and beyond.

articles.title: Kenya loses $92 million in AfDB shares after missing critical payment
articles.summary: Kenya has forfeited African Development Bank (AfDB) shares worth approximately $92 million after failing to make its annual subscription payment. This development comes at a time when Kenya’s borrowing from the bank has risen significantly, raising concerns that the loss will severely weaken its clout and influence in the bank’s governance and decision-making processes. According […]
articles.context: Kenya's forfeiture of $92 million in AfDB shares follows a history of increased borrowing from the bank, which reached $3.4 billion in 2022, primarily for infrastructure projects. This situation is exacerbated by ongoing economic challenges, including high public debt and inflation, prompting concerns over Kenya's diminishing influence in AfDB governance amid its critical role in regional development. The missed payment, due in September 2023, highlights the urgency of Kenya's fiscal management.

articles.title: African island states seek stronger credit ratings for climate and blue economy finance
articles.summary: Experts, policymakers, and financial practitioners from across African Island States will gather in Mombasa on 12–13 June 2026 for a high-level workshop to enhance sovereign credit ratings and unlock access to sustainable climate and blue economy financing. Jointly organized by the Economic Commission for Africa (ECA) and the African Island States Coordination Committee (AISCC) under […]
articles.context: The African Island States, particularly those in the Indian Ocean, face significant challenges due to climate change and rising sea levels, threatening their economies and livelihoods. The 2021 United Nations Climate Change Conference (COP26) highlighted the urgent need for financing to support climate resilience. In this context, the upcoming workshop in Mombasa aims to address the financial barriers these nations face, particularly after the 2023 G7 summit emphasized the importance of sustainable financing for vulnerable states.

articles.title: Why Ethiopia should stop calling road crashes “Accidents”
articles.summary: The night of May 30, 2026 was an important night for football fans across the globe. English Premiership champions Arsenal battled French League One Champions Paris Saint Germain for the UEFA Champions League final. This match had a particularly high anticipation in Addis Ababa where there is a large Arsenal fan base. The week of […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with a surge in road fatalities, exacerbated by ongoing political instability and inadequate infrastructure. The government’s reluctance to address these incidents as preventable tragedies, instead labeling them as "accidents," reflects a broader issue of accountability amidst the Tigray conflict and civil unrest. As the nation seeks to rebuild and stabilize, the framing of road safety issues becomes crucial in fostering a culture of responsibility and change.

articles.title: Name:Rahel Nigussie
articles.summary: 2 Education : ( የት/ት ደረጃ) 10 3 Company Name ( የካምፓኒ ስም) Rich Coffee 4 Title; : ( የስራ ድርሻህ) Owner 5 Founded in; ( መቼ ተመሰረተ ) 2013 6 What it do; (ምንድነው የምትሰሩት)Coffee and Tea services 7 Head quarter ( መስሪያቤት )Addis Ababa 8 Start up capital ( በምን ያህል ገንዘብ ስራዉን […]
articles.context: Rahel Nigussie's Rich Coffee, founded in 2013, emerges at a time when Ethiopia's coffee industry faces challenges due to ongoing political unrest and economic instability. The government's efforts to promote local businesses amid the Tigray conflict and recent reforms aimed at liberalizing the economy highlight the significance of entrepreneurial ventures like Rich Coffee in fostering resilience and growth in the sector. This context underscores the importance of supporting small businesses in a rapidly changing landscape.

articles.title: Invest in Ethiopian cities startup ecosystems for a transformational and sustainable private sector led economy
articles.summary: Government versus Private Sector Led Economies State led command and mixed economies are characterized by government owning major means of production. The state allocates resources via directs investments. The government directly controls prices be it for inputs, goods, services, Foreign Exchange (FX) or interest rates. They feature dominance of State Owned Enterprises (SOEs) and substantial […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's economy has historically been dominated by state-owned enterprises, particularly under the leadership of the Prosperity Party since 2018. The ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which erupted in November 2020, has exacerbated economic challenges, leading to calls for a shift towards a more private sector-driven economy. Recent reforms, including the 2021 privatization of Ethio Telecom, highlight the urgency for investment in urban startup ecosystems to foster sustainable growth amidst these turbulent dynamics.

articles.title: Prosperity gospel, false prophets and the money scam
articles.summary: Religion has historically functioned as a source of moral guidance, spiritual meaning, and social cohesion. Across centuries, faith institutions have offered comfort to the poor, challenged injustice, and shaped ethical values within society. Yet religion has also repeatedly been exploited by individuals who weaponise belief for personal gain. In contemporary Christianity, the rise of the […]
articles.context: In Ethiopia, the rise of the Prosperity Gospel has gained traction amid political instability and economic hardship, particularly following the 2018 reforms that led to increased religious freedom. This shift has allowed charismatic leaders, often labeled as false prophets, to exploit vulnerable populations. The ongoing ethnic tensions and conflict, especially in regions like Tigray, have further exacerbated the situation, as desperate individuals seek solace and hope in these new religious movements.

articles.title: 'It was either killed or be killed' - ongoing nightmares of an ex-child soldier in Somalia
articles.summary: Yusuf Ali, now 34, struggles with his memories living in Mogadishu surrounded by dark reminders of his past.
articles.context: Somalia has faced decades of civil war since the overthrow of President Siad Barre in 1991, leading to the rise of various militant groups, including Al-Shabaab, which continues to recruit child soldiers. The ongoing conflict has intensified with the recent military offensive against Al-Shabaab by the Somali National Army and African Union forces, particularly following the 2022 elections that aimed to stabilize the country. This backdrop highlights the enduring trauma faced by former child soldiers like Yusuf Ali.

articles.title: Drone strike on market kills 11 civilians in Sudan’s North Kordofan: Rights group
articles.summary: Dozens also injured in 2nd drone attack in area within 24 hours, Emergency Lawyers group says
articles.context: The drone strike in North Kordofan comes amid escalating violence in Sudan following the outbreak of conflict in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces. This ongoing power struggle has exacerbated humanitarian crises, with civilian areas increasingly targeted. The recent attacks highlight the deteriorating security situation and the urgent need for international intervention, especially as the UN reported over 5 million people displaced since the conflict began.

articles.title: Mauritius bans entry of travelers from DR Congo, Uganda, South Sudan over Ebola concerns
articles.summary: Cabinet also proposes postponement of US-Africa business summit scheduled to be hosted in July
articles.context: Mauritius' decision to ban travelers from the Democratic Republic of Congo, Uganda, and South Sudan comes amid heightened concerns over the Ebola outbreak that has affected these countries, particularly in the context of the ongoing humanitarian crisis in South Sudan, where conflict has exacerbated health vulnerabilities. The proposed postponement of the US-Africa business summit, originally scheduled for July 2023, reflects the delicate balance between health security and international diplomatic engagement in the region.

articles.title: NEBE Sets Deadline for Parties to File Detailed Grievances
articles.summary: The National Election Board of Ethiopia (NEBE) on Friday urged all political parties that participated in the country’s seventh general election to submit complete and consolidated complaints related to voting, vote counting and the announcement of results by June 6. The boarsaid it had received complaints through election-day grievance desks, its early warning and response […]
articles.context: The NEBE's call for grievances comes in the wake of Ethiopia's contentious seventh general election held on August 21, 2021, which was marred by allegations of irregularities and violence, particularly in the Tigray region. The ongoing conflict between the federal government and Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) has heightened tensions, complicating the political landscape as various parties seek to address perceived injustices and ensure electoral integrity amidst a fragile peace process initiated by the November 2022 ceasefire agreement.

articles.title: From Zero to Hero: Senegalese Midfielder Ndiaye’s Breathtaking Sx-month Journey to Top
articles.summary: BERLIN – Senegalese international Bara Sapoko Ndiaye might still feel caught in the middle of a strange journey after an unprecedented upswing at light speed. The 18-year-old’s story
articles.context: Senegal's football scene has gained prominence following the national team's victory at the 2021 Africa Cup of Nations, which has heightened interest in young talents like Ndiaye. Amidst ongoing political unrest in Ethiopia, where sports often serve as a unifying force, Ndiaye's rapid ascent symbolizes hope and resilience for many in the region. His journey reflects the broader narrative of African youth striving for success in international sports despite challenging circumstances.

articles.title: Africa CDC, WHO Launch Joint $518m Response Plan for Ebola Outbreak
articles.summary: African Union and United Nations health agencies on Friday launched a joint continental preparedness and response plan for the ongoing Ebola outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus. The
articles.context: The ongoing Ebola outbreak, first identified in Uganda in 2007, has raised alarms due to its potential spread across borders, particularly in the Horn of Africa, where health systems are already strained by conflicts and humanitarian crises. Ethiopia, as a key regional player and host to the African Union, is pivotal in coordinating responses. The recent launch of the $518 million plan by the Africa CDC and WHO underscores the urgency of addressing health security amid rising tensions in the region.

articles.title: Russian language day celebrated at UNECA ahead of global observanceBy our staff reporter
articles.summary: The headquarters of the United Nations Economic Commission for Africa (UNECA) hosted a high-profile diplomatic and cultural event on June 4, 2026, to mark Russian Language Day. Held ahead of the official global observance on June 6, the event was noted for strengthening the growing diplomatic, educational, and cultural ties between the Russian Federation and […]
articles.context: The celebration of Russian Language Day at UNECA comes amid Ethiopia's efforts to diversify its international partnerships, particularly following the 2020 Tigray conflict that strained relations with Western nations. The event highlights Ethiopia's growing ties with Russia, which have been reinforced through agreements on military cooperation and economic collaboration since 2021, as the country seeks to bolster its geopolitical standing in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: MPs Ratify Bilateral Air Transport Deals with 3 African nations, Bangladesh
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – The House of People’s Representatives (HoPR) has ratified bilateral air transport service agreements concluded with three African countries and Bangladesh. Fethi Mahdi (PhD), deputy chair
articles.context: The ratification of these bilateral air transport agreements comes amid Ethiopia's efforts to strengthen regional ties following the 2020 Tigray conflict, which strained relations with neighboring countries. The agreements with Kenya, Uganda, and Tanzania, as well as Bangladesh, are part of Ethiopia's broader strategy to enhance economic cooperation and boost tourism, particularly as the country seeks to recover from the pandemic's impact on its aviation sector.

articles.title: UN chief alarmed by reports of violence in Somalia's capital
articles.summary: Guterres calls for restraint and urges all parties to avoid actions that could fuel further violence
articles.context: Somalia has faced decades of instability since the collapse of its central government in 1991, leading to a power vacuum exploited by various militant groups, including Al-Shabaab. The recent escalation of violence follows the controversial presidential election in May 2022, which has deepened political divisions. The ongoing conflict is further complicated by humanitarian crises and regional tensions, particularly with neighboring countries like Ethiopia and Kenya, which have vested interests in Somalia's stability.

articles.title: iibGroup Expands Chevening Partnership to Ethiopia as Part of Broader Regional Engagement
articles.summary: Building on its successful partnership with the Chevening Scholarships programme in Cabo Verde and Djibouti, iibGroup has renewed its support for the 2026–2027 academic year and expanded the initiative to Ethiopia. This year, three fully funded Chevening scholarships will be awarded across Africa, one each in Cabo Verde, Djibouti, and Ethiopia, reinforcing iib’s commitment to […]
articles.context: The expansion of iibGroup's partnership with the Chevening Scholarships to Ethiopia comes amid ongoing political reforms and a fragile peace following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a ceasefire agreement in November 2022. As Ethiopia seeks to rebuild and strengthen its international ties, this initiative aims to foster educational opportunities and enhance regional collaboration, particularly in a context where access to higher education remains limited due to socio-political challenges.

articles.title: EthSwitch records 1 million daily EthioPay-IPS transactions, wins African financial inclusion award

The achievement comes as EthSwitch and global payment software company BPC received The Asian […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been undergoing significant economic reforms since Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed took office in 2018, aiming to modernize its financial sector and enhance digital payment systems. The recent surge in EthioPay-IPS transactions highlights the government's push for financial inclusion amid ongoing challenges, including political tensions and conflict in regions like Tigray. This milestone coincides with Ethiopia's efforts to attract foreign investment and improve its economic stability following years of unrest.

articles.title: Heavy gunfire in Somali capital as row over election delay escalates
articles.summary: The opposition has called for protests after the president's term in office was extended by a year.
articles.context: The recent escalation follows President Mohamed Abdullahi Farmaajo's controversial decision to extend his term by a year on April 12, 2021, amid stalled elections originally scheduled for February 2021. This move has intensified tensions between the federal government and opposition leaders, including former presidents Hassan Sheikh Mohamud and Sharif Sheikh Ahmed, who argue that the extension undermines democratic processes and risks reigniting clan-based violence in a country already grappling with Al-Shabaab insurgency.

articles.title: Ethiopia Reaches Staff-Level Deal with IMF to unlock $468 million loan
articles.summary: Ethiopia has reached a staff level agreement with the International Monetary Fund (IMF) on the fifth review of its $3.4 billion economic program, the fund said on Wednesday,
articles.context: Ethiopia's agreement with the IMF comes amid ongoing economic challenges exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020, leading to significant humanitarian and financial crises. The country is also navigating a complex political landscape following the peace agreement signed in November 2022, aimed at stabilizing the region and fostering economic recovery. This staff-level deal is crucial for unlocking funds that could support Ethiopia's recovery efforts and bolster investor confidence.

articles.title: Election Conducted Peacefully Despite challenges: AU, Igad Observers
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – Ethiopia’s seventh general election, held on Monday, was marked by high voter turnout as well as orderly and peaceful participation, according to preliminary reports by
articles.context: Ethiopia's seventh general election comes amid a fragile peace following the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020 and lasted until the signing of a peace agreement in November 2022. The political landscape is further complicated by ongoing tensions in the Oromia and Amhara regions, as well as the role of the African Union (AU) and Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD) in promoting stability. articles.title: Ethiopian Airlines Set to Resume Flights to Kuwait
articles.summary: Ethiopian Airlines is set to resume its passenger flight services to Kuwait in mid July after months of hiatus caused by the conflict in the Middle East. The

The resumption of flights in mid-July comes as diplomatic efforts, including the recent normalization talks between Israel and several Arab nations, aim to stabilize the region. This move reflects Ethiopia's strategic interest in maintaining its connections to the Gulf amid shifting geopolitical dynamics.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s tight monetary policy wins IMF praise as key to inflation fight, paving way for nearly $500 million disbursement
articles.summary: Ethiopia’s commitment to a tight monetary policy stance—hailed by international partners as a critical safeguard against rising inflation—has taken center stage as the country secured a staff-level agreement with the International Monetary Fund (IMF) for the fifth review of its economic reform program. An IMF staff team led by Alvaro Piris visited Addis Ababa from […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant economic challenges, including soaring inflation and currency depreciation, exacerbated by the Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020. The government’s commitment to a tight monetary policy is part of its broader economic reform agenda initiated in 2021, which aims to stabilize the economy and attract foreign investment. The recent staff-level agreement with the IMF, reached in October 2023, marks a crucial step in securing nearly $500 million in financial assistance.

articles.title: US says Kenya Ebola facility poses no risk amid growing controversy

articles.context: The establishment of the Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya has sparked controversy amid rising public opposition and a legal challenge from local communities concerned about health risks. This comes in the wake of the 2014 Ebola outbreak in West Africa, which heightened global awareness of infectious disease threats. Additionally, Kenya's ongoing political tensions, particularly following the contentious 2022 elections, have amplified distrust towards government initiatives and foreign involvement in public health matters.

articles.title: Two people shot dead amid Kenya protests against US Ebola quarantine centre plan
articles.summary: The US plan has sparked public anger and led to demonstrations near the site of the proposed treatment facility.
articles.context: The protests in Kenya are rooted in a broader distrust of foreign intervention in public health, exacerbated by the 2014 Ebola outbreak in West Africa and the perceived failures of international responses. The proposed US Ebola quarantine center, announced in late 2023, has reignited fears of colonialism and exploitation, particularly in light of Kenya's ongoing struggles with governance and public health infrastructure. This situation is further complicated by the political tensions surrounding the upcoming 2024 general elections.

articles.title: "النفاذ إلى البحر الأحمر": فصل جديد في صراع مصر وإثيوبيا
articles.summary: يتزايد صراع النفوذ في القرن الأفريقي بين مصر وإثيوبيا، فبعد أن شهدت العلاقات بين البلدين توتراً كبيراً بسبب سد النهضة الإثيوبي، انتقل الصراع من مياه النيل إلى البحر الأحمر.

articles.title: Ghana warns citizens against non-essential travel to South Africa over anti-immigrant attacks

This context complicates the response efforts of health workers on the ground.

Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's Prosperity Party, which has faced criticism for its handling of the conflict, is nonetheless expected to secure a significant electoral victory amid these tensions.

As President William Ruto's administration grapples with rising insecurity and public discontent, the march highlights the urgent need for a national response to these escalating crises.

The elections, originally scheduled for 2020, were postponed due to the pandemic and security issues, making this vote critical for national stability.

The recent arrests of students reflect heightened scrutiny on youth behavior and accountability in a country grappling with rising cases of school unrest and violence.

As Ethiopia is one of the world's largest coffee producers, Sori's efforts to share her heritage through coffee highlight the resilience of Ethiopian culture and the importance of coffee in fostering community ties amidst adversity.

articles.summary: الخرطوم 19 مارس 2026 – قال قائد الجيش السوداني ورئيس مجلس السيادة عبد الفتاح البرهان، الخميس، إنه يمكن التعاطي مع أي مبادرة سلام مع قوات الدعم السريع وفقا لعدة شروط. وأكد البرهان في خطاب وجهه للسودانيين بمناسبة عيد الفطر ليل الخميس، أن قيادة الدولة حريصةً على التعاطي الإيجابي والمسؤول مع كل مبادرةٍ صادقة لسلام، لكن بما يحقق الأمن ولا يُعيد إنتاج الحرب ولا يُبقي على أسبابها أو أدواتها. وأضاف البرهان أن على الوسطاء والساعون إلى إنهاء الحرب النهوض بمسؤولياتهم والمساعدة في إنفاذ مبادرات السلام التي قدّمتها الحكومة السودانية. وتابع قائلا: “إن مبادراتٌ الحكومة تنطلق من إرادة السودانيين وتستجيب لمطلبهم العادل في تفكيك وإنهاء أي دورٍ سياسي أو...

articles.summary: الطينة 18 مارس 2026 لقي 16 مدنياً تشادياً على الأقل حتفهم، وأصيب آخرون إثر هجوم شنته طائرة مسيّرة يُرجح تبعيتها للدعم السريع، استهدف تجمعاً للمواطنين في بلدة “مبروكة” التابعة لبلدة الطينة في إقليم “وادي فيرا” التشادي، فيما وجه الرئيس التشادي بالرد على أي هجوم قادم من السودان. ويعد هذا الهجوم الرابع من نوعه خلال الأشهر الأخيرة، بعد أن توغلت قوة من الدعم السريع في يناير الماضي داخل الأراضي التشادية واستهدفت معسكراً للجيش، مما أسفر عن مقتل ثمانية جنود. وأعلنت الدعم السريع وقتها أن الهجوم وقع عن طريق الخطأ وقدمت اعتذارها للحكومة التشادية. ويتزامن هجوم اليوم الأربعاء مع المواجهات العسكرية الضارية التي تشهدها “الطينة” هذا الأسبوع، حيث يتصدى...

articles.title: «أدري».. من شريان حياة إلى بوابة مؤصدة: جحيم الحصار يطارد نازحي دارفور
articles.summary: أدري 18 مارس 2026- أصدرت الحكومة التشادية في فبراير الماضي قراراً فاجأ آلاف النازحين السودانيين العالقين على حدودها الشرقية، بإعلانها إغلاق جميع المعابر الحدودية مع السودان “حتى إشعار آخر”. وكان منفذ “أدري”، الذي يعد الشريان الرئيسي الذي يعبره الفارون من جحيم الحرب في دارفور، أول المغلَقين. لم يكن القرار تشادياً خالصاً، بل جاء تتويجاً لأسابيع من التوتر المتصاعد على الحدود، أدت إلى سقوط قتلى في صفوف الجنود التشاديين. وبين ليلة وضحاها، تحول معبر الأمل إلى بوابة مؤصدة، تاركاً مئات العائلات عالقة في العراء، بين جحيم الحرب خلفها وبوابة مغلقة أمامها.

articles.title: أطفال السودان في رمضان.. طفولة سرقتها الحرب
articles.summary: منتدى الإعلام السوداني بورتسودان، مدني، 18 مارس 2026 (سودان تربيون)– لم تكن آمنة محمد تتخيل أن يأتي رمضان يومًا وهي تحاول إقناع أطفالها بأن القليل من الطعام يكفي للإفطار. نزحت آمنة مع أطفالها الثلاثة من منزلهم في الخرطوم قبل ثلاث سنوات، بعد اشتداد القتال بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع، لتجد نفسها في أحد مراكز الإيواء بمدينة بورتسودان، حيث تغيرت ملامح الشهر الكريم تمامًا بالنسبة لأطفالها. تقول آمنة بصوت يختلط فيه الحزن بالصبر: “كان أطفالي ينتظرون رمضان كل عام لشراء الفوانيس والملابس الجديدة، لكن هذا العام لا نفكر إلا في توفير الطعام. أحيانًا لا نجد ما يكفي للإفطار.

articles.title: ايصال أدوية لدعم (80) ألف طفل في «طويلة» غرب السودان
articles.summary: طويلة 18 مارس 2026 – أعلنت منظمة أنقذوا الأطفال، الأربعاء، عن إيصال أدوية لدعم 80 ألف طفل و57 ألف بالغ لمدة 6 أشهر في طويلة بولاية شمال دارفور غربي السودان. وتحوّلت طويلة، الخاضعة لسيطرة حركة تحرير السودان بقيادة عبد الواحد نور، من بلدات نائية تفتقر إلى الخدمات إلى مأوى لأكثر من 715 ألف نازح، معظمهم فرّوا من الهجمات على الفاشر ومخيمات النزوح المجاورة لها. وقالت المنظمة، في بيان، إنها أوصلت “شحنة من بورتسودان إلى طويلة لدعم أكثر من 80 ألف طفل و57 ألف بالغ في طويلة، حيث إنها كافية للإبقاء على عمل 20 مرفقًا صحيًا وعيادات متنقلة تخدم المجتمعات النازحة لمدة ستة أشهر”. وأشارت إلى أن الشحنة، التي تحمل 30 طنًا من الأدوية وإمدادات التغذية، وصلت...

articles.title: شبكة أطباء السودان تتهم «الدعم السريع »بتصفية مدنيين قرب بارا
articles.summary: بارا 18 مارس 2026 – قالت شبكة أطباء السودان، الأربعاء، إن قوات الدعم السريع اغتالت 12 مدنيا بينهم نساء وأصابت 3 آخرين بذريعة الانتماء للجيش، في منطقة (شريم ميما) شمالي مدينة بارا بولاية شمال كردفان. وتبادل كل من الجيش السوداني والدعم السريع السيطرة على بارا ثاني أكبر مدن ولاية شمال كردفان عدة مرات، قبل أن تسيطر عليها قوات الدعم السريع هذا الأسبوع. وأفادت الشبكة في بيان، إن “الدعم السريع ارتكبت مجزرة جديدة في منطقة شريم ميما شمالي مدينة بارا، حيث صفّت 12 شخصا بينهم 6 نساء، وأصابت 3 آخرين بجروح متفاوتة، بتهمة انتمائهم إلى الجيش السوداني”. وأشارت الشبكة إلى أن استهداف المدنيين العزّل يمثل انتهاكا صارخا للقوانين والأعراف الإنسانية والدولية.

articles.title: اتهامات للدعم السريع بإحراق قافلة محاصيل غرب النهود
articles.summary: النهود 17 مارس 2026- أقدمت قوات الدعم السريع، الثلاثاء، على إحراق قافلة تجارية تضم شاحنات كبيرة محملة بمحاصيل زراعية بالقرب من مدينة النهود بولاية غرب كردفان، أثناء توجهها إلى الأبيض بولاية شمال كردفان. ووثق عناصر من القوات عبر مقطع فيديو لحظة إضرام النار في الشاحنات التي كانت محملة بكميات كبيرة من المحاصيل الزراعية، بما في ذلك الذرة والفول السوداني. وتُعد النهود وغبيش وودبندة بولاية غرب كردفان من أبرز مناطق إنتاج الفول في السودان، كما تضم النهود واحدة من أكبر بورصات تداول المحاصيل في غرب السودان، غير أنها تعرضت لعمليات نهب واسعة عقب سيطرة الدعم السريع على المنطقة، الأمر الذي أدى إلى اضطراب النشاط التجاري ودفع العديد من التجار إلى مغادرة...

articles.title: السودان : معدل التضخم يواصل التراجع
articles.summary: الخرطوم17 مارس 2026 – كشف جهاز الإحصاء المركزي في السودان، الثلاثاء، عن تراجع معدل التضخم في فبراير 2026 إلى 56% مقارنة بالشهر الذي سبقه. وبدأ معدل التضخم ينخفض تدريجيًا بعد أن بلغ أعلى مستوياته 422% في يوليو 2021، رغم الانخفاض المتوالي في قيمة العملة المحلية مقابل العملات الأخرى وارتفاع أسعار السلع، حيث سجل 60.26% في يناير الماضي. وقال جهاز الإحصاء، في بيان، إن “معدل التغير في المستوى العام للأسعار – التضخم – في شهر فبراير 2026 زاد بنسبة 56.39% عن نظيره في العام السابق”. وأشار إلى أن معدل التضخم في المناطق الحضرية في شهر فبراير بلغ 72.12%، فيما وصل معدله في المناطق الريفية إلى 54.08%.

articles.title: «بنك السودان»: عجز الميزان التجاري في 2025 بلغ 3.8 مليار دولار
articles.summary: الخرطوم، 16 مارس 2026 – كشف بنك السودان المركزي، الاثنين، عن اتساع العجز في الميزان التجاري للسودان خلال العام الماضي إلى 3.8 مليار دولار. وظل السودان يسجل عجزًا في الميزان التجاري كل عام، يتراوح بين 3 إلى 6 مليارات دولار، قبل أن ينخفض في 2024 إلى 1.7 مليار دولار. وقال البنك المركزي، في تقرير الموجز الإحصائي للتجارة الخارجية لعام 2025، إن “أجمالي الصادرات السودانية بلغ نحو 2.64 مليار دولار، مقابل واردات بلغت 6.49 مليار دولار، مما أدى إلى تسجيل عجز تجاري قدره نحو 3.86 مليار دولار”. وأوضح أن قيمة الصادرات البالغة 2.639 مليار دولار جاءت نتيجة هيمنة قطاع المعادن والمنتجات الزراعية والثروة الحيوانية.

articles.title: «الدعم السريع» تسيطر على بارا بشمال كردفان وكرنوي بشمال دارفور
articles.summary: بارا، 16 مارس 2026 – سيطرت قوات الدعم السريع مرة أخرى، الإثنين، على مدينة بارا ثاني أكبر مدن ولاية شمال كردفان وعلى بلدة كرنوي الحدودية مع تشاد بولاية شمال دارفور. وتقع بارا على بعد نحو 45 كيلومتر شرقي الأبيض عاصمة الولاية، بينما تقع كرنوي ضمن آخر المعاقل الإثنية للقوة المشتركة للحركات التي تقاتل إلى جانب الجيش السوداني. ومنذ اندلاع الحرب بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع في أبريل 2023 سيطرت قوات الدعم السريع على مدينة بارا، ومن ثم استعادها الجيش في سبتمبر 2025. لكن قوات الدعم السريع سيطرت على المدينة مجددا في أكتوبر من العام الماضي، ثم استعادها الجيش قبل 10 أيام، قبل أن تعلن قوات الدعم السريع اليوم الإثنين الاستيلاء على بارا.

articles.summary: الخرطوم 15 مارس 2026 – قرر رئيس الوزراء كامل إدريس الغاء وكالة التخطيط الاقتصادي بوزارة المالية في خطوة تهدف إلى جذب المنح والقروض الدولية َوترقية الأداء الاقتصادي. وشهدت وكالة التخطيط الاقتصادي بوزارة المالية السودانية خلال السنوات الماضية حالات متكررة من الهيكلة، الإلغاء، وإعادة الإنشاء كجزء من محاولات تطوير الهياكل الداخلية لضبط الأداء الاقتصادي. وأصدر رئيس الوزراء، كامل إدريس الأحد “قراراً قضى بإلغاء وكالة التخطيط بوزارة المالية ودمج كامل مهامها واختصاصاتها ضمن الأمانة العامة للمجلس القومي للتخطيط الاستراتيجي”. كما نص القرار في شقه الإداري على إعفاء محمد بشار محمد آدم من منصب وكيل التخطيط بوزارة المالية، في خطوة تعكس توجهاً جديداً...

articles.title: السلطات السودانية تعتقل القيادي الإسلامي الناجي عبد الله
articles.summary: الخرطوم 15 مارس 2026 – اعتقلت السلطات في السودان، الأحد، القيادي الإسلامي البارز الناجي عبد الله، وفقاً لما أفاد به أحد قادة المقاومة الشعبية. وظل الناجي عبد الله وهو أحد قيادات المقاومة الشعبية التي تقاتل إلى جانب الجيش في حربه ضد قوات الدعم السريع يجاهر بمواقفه المؤيدة للقضية الفلسطينية وداعماً لحركة المقاومة الإسلامية “حماس”. وأعلن الناجي في الثالث من مارس الجاري، خلال مخاطبته إفطاراً رمضانياً أمام حشد من مجموعات عسكرية محسوبة على التيار الإسلامي، دعمهم لإيران في الحرب التي تقودها ضد إسرائيل والولايات المتحدة الأميركية. وأثارت تلك التصريحات ردود فعل واسعة حيث أعلن القائد العام للجيش عبد الفتاح البرهان تبرؤه من تلك المجموعة، مؤكداً...

articles.title: «كبر» يكذب صحة مقطع فيديو يتحدث عن «البرهان» أثار جدلًا واسعًا
articles.summary: الخرطوم 16 مارس 2026 – نفى رئيس شورى المؤتمر الوطني – المحلول، والي شمال دارفور الأسبق عثمان محمد يوسف كبر، فجر الاثنين، صحة مقطع فيديو أثار جدلًا واسعًا في السودان تحدث خلاله عن رئيس مجلس السيادة وقائد الجيش عبد الفتاح البرهان. ونشرت قناة “اسكاي نيوز عربية” الإماراتية، الأحد، تقريرًا حوى مقطع فيديو يظهر كبر جالسًا على كرسي مريح يتحدث إلى أشخاص قربه في جلسة يبدو أنها غير رسمية، دون أن تُظهر اللقطات هؤلاء الأشخاص الجالسين قربه. وقال كبر، في مقطع الفيديو، إنهم “تحدثوا للبرهان لتسلميه المجلس العسكري لسحق المتظاهرين وتسليم السلطة إلى الإسلاميين، وهو لم يكن له دور، وما كان محسوبًا ضابطًا، حيث كان يجلس مثل الحرس خارج الباب”. وقال كبر، في بيان،...

articles.title: الصين تعلن  الاستعداد لمساعدة السودان في إعادة الإعمار
articles.summary: بورتسودان 15 مارس 2026 – أعلن القائم بأعمال السفارة الصينية في السودان شيوي جيان، الأحد، وضع مقترحات لمساعدة الخرطوم في إعادة الإعمار ضمن سياسات بكين الخارجية. وتعرضت مدن عديدة، بما في ذلك العاصمة الخرطوم، لدمار واسع طال المؤسسات الخدمية مثل المياه والكهرباء والصحة، إضافة إلى البنية التحتية والمصانع، علاوة على تأثير النزاع على الأنشطة الزراعية والثروة الحيوانية. وقال شيوي جيان، في تنوير صحفي بمدينة بورتسودان شرقي البلاد، إن الصين “على استعداد للمساهمة في إعمار ما دمرته الحرب بالسودان”. وأشار إلى أن بكين ضمنت مقترحات بشأن هذه المساهمة في سياساتها الخارجية للفترة المقبلة.
